root_cause.text: The cdxl_decode_frame function incorrectly sets the pixel format to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 for the CHUNKY format, leading to an out-of-bounds array access (heap-based buffer overflow).
root_cause.evidence: The patch modifies the condition `if (!encoding && c->palette_size && c->bpp <= 8)` to include `&& c->format != CHUNKY`, explicitly preventing the CHUNKY format from triggering the PAL8 pixel format assignment.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The cdxl_decode_frame function incorrectly sets the pixel format to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 for the CHUNKY format, leading to an out-of-bounds array access (heap-based buffer overflow).
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch modifies the condition `if (!encoding && c->palette_size && c->bpp <= 8)` to include `&& c->format != CHUNKY`, explicitly preventing the CHUNKY format from triggering the PAL8 pixel format assignment.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix incorrect pixel format assignment for chunky data by adding a format check.
intent_record.what: Added a condition to check that c->format is not CHUNKY before setting the pixel format to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8.
intent_record.how: Prevents the decoder from incorrectly assigning a paletted pixel format when processing chunky format data.

grouping_result.intent: Fix incorrect pixel format assignment for chunky data by adding a format check.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly fixes the incorrect pixel format assignment for chunky data, which is the exact root cause of the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This condition incorrectly allows setting the pixel format to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 without checking if the format is CHUNKY, which causes the vulnerability.
This assignment incorrectly sets the pixel format to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 for the CHUNKY format, leading to the out-of-bounds array access.

vuln_statements.explanation: This condition is missing a check for the CHUNKY format, incorrectly allowing the execution to enter the block and set the pixel format to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 for CHUNKY data.
This assignment incorrectly sets the pixel format to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 for CHUNKY format data due to the insufficient check above, leading to the out-of-bounds access.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
cdxl: set pix_fmt PAL8 only if palette is available

Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Justi
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 126daeb5507425d5e9906731d70015d79045d131
Author: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
Date:   Sat Feb 25 17:16:39 2012 +0000

    cdxl: set pix_fmt PAL8 only if palette is available
    
    Signed-off-by: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
    Signed-off-by: Justin Ruggles <justin.ruggles@gmail.com>

diff --git a/libavcodec/cdxl.c b/libavcodec/cdxl.c
index 01ebbccc19..e23b93421a 100644
--- a/libavcodec/cdxl.c
+++ b/libavcodec/cdxl.c
@@ -213,7 +213,7 @@ static int cdxl_decode_frame(AVCodecContext
bic_trace_step.reason: The diff clearly shows the fix being applied, changing the unconditional 'if (encoding == 0)' to 'if (!encoding && c->palette_size && c->bpp <= 8)' before setting the pixel format to PIX_FMT_PAL8. This prevents the out-of-bounds access when the palette is not available.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 716d413c13981da15323c7a3821860536eefdbbb
Author: Anton Khirnov <anton@khirnov.net>
Date:   Sat Oct 6 12:10:34 2012 +0200

    Replace PIX_FMT_* -> AV_PIX_FMT_*, PixelFormat -> AVPixelFormat

diff --git a/libavcodec/cdxl.c b/libavcodec/cdxl.c
index 86b7515df9..bc4dfe74fc 100644
--- a/libavcodec/cdxl.c
+++ b/libavcodec/cdxl.c
@@ -247,11 +247,11 @@ static int cdxl_decode_frame(AVCodecContext *avctx, void *data,
     if (c->video_size < aligned_width * avctx->height * c->bpp / 8)
         ret
bic_trace_step.reason: The function cdxl_decode_frame sets avctx->pix_fmt = AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 when '!encoding && c->palette_size && c->bpp <= 8', but it lacks the necessary check to ensure this is only done for the correct format (e.g. not for CHUNKY format), which is the root cause of the vulnerability. The commit merely renames PIX_FMT_PAL8 to AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 without fixing the logic.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit dc4e57489fa0f9cf4faf4c85cc405d6db77d84db
Author: Paul B Mahol <onemda@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Feb 14 17:36:20 2012 +0000

    CDXL demuxer and decoder

    Signed-off-by: Diego Biurrun <diego@biurrun.de>

diff --git a/libavcodec/cdxl.c b/libavcodec/cdxl.c
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..a53a001ea4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/libavcodec/cdxl.c
@@ -0,0 +1,278 @@
+/*
+ * CDXL video decoder
+ * Copyright (c) 2011-2012 Paul B Mahol
+ *
+ * T
bic_trace_step.reason: The code currently explicitly rejects any format other than 0 (which includes the CHUNKY format) with 'unsupported pixel format: %d\n'. Therefore, the vulnerability where AV_PIX_FMT_PAL8 is incorrectly set for the CHUNKY format is not yet present in this version, as the CHUNKY format itself is not supported or processed.
